description:
You are acting as a backup-administrator who has just been asked to prepare a
    clean error-only extract of last month’s application logs and to archive the
    original data together with your extract and a machine-readable report that
    management will parse automatically.

    Initial data that already exist on the system  
      • /home/user/source_logs/  
        ├── app_server.log  
        ├── app_worker.log  
        ├── app_2022_old.log  
        ├── system.log  
        └── readme.txt (plain-text helper file, NOT a “.log”)  

    Task – produce three artefacts

    1.  Directory “/home/user/backup_2023” must be created.  
        All new output files must live **inside** this directory.

    2.  Filtered error extract  
        File name:  /home/user/backup_2023/filtered_errors.txt  
        Content rule:  
          • Inspect every file located in “/home/user/source_logs/” whose
            **file-name ends with “.log” AND begins with “app”** (shell-style
            pattern “app*.log”).  
          • Copy **only** the lines that contain the literal substring
            “ERROR”.  
          • Process the matching files in alphabetical order and **append**
            error lines in the exact order they already appear in each file.
          • The resulting file therefore contains one text line per error with
            no additional headers or separators.
          • Lines that originated from non-matching files (e.g. “system.log”)
            must never appear in this extract.

    3.  Machine-readable backup report  
        File name:  /home/user/backup_2023/backup_report.log  
        Strict line format (pipe-separated, no spaces):  
            <filename>|<original_linecount>|<error_linecount>|<status>  
          • <filename>            – just the basename, e.g. “app_server.log”  
          • <original_linecount>  – total number of lines in the file  
          • <error_linecount>     – number of lines that contained “ERROR”  
          • <status>              – exactly “included” if the file matched
                                    the “app*.log” pattern, otherwise “skipped”  
          • One such line is required for **every** “*.log” file present in
            /home/user/source_logs/, listed in alphabetical order.
          • After those per-file lines, append a single summary line:
              TOTAL|<total_log_files>|<total_included>|<total_error_lines>|<timestamp>
              where  
                – <total_log_files>  = count of “*.log” files processed  
                – <total_included>   = how many were flagged “included”  
                – <total_error_lines>= cumulative ERROR lines copied into
                                      filtered_errors.txt  
                – <timestamp>        = current local time in the exact ISO-8601
                                      format “YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS” (24-hour,
                                      no timezone suffix).  
          • Ensure there is **no trailing whitespace** on any line.

    4.  Compressed archive  
        File name:  /home/user/backup_2023.tar.gz (gzip-compressed tar)  
        Must contain, at its top level, the following two members:  
          a) complete directory “source_logs” with every file exactly as it
             existed under /home/user/source_logs  
          b) the directory “backup_2023” with both newly-created files
             “filtered_errors.txt” and “backup_report.log”  

    5.  Verification expectations  
        The automated test will confirm, among other things, that:  
          • /home/user/backup_2023 exists and contains only the two files
            specified above, no extras.  
          • “filtered_errors.txt” contains exactly the ERROR lines extracted
            according to the rules and in the correct order.  
          • “backup_report.log” follows the pipe-delimited format strictly and
            ends with the TOTAL line whose numeric fields and timestamp format
            are correct.  
          • “backup_2023.tar.gz” can be extracted without errors and includes
            the exact directory/file hierarchy described.

    Produce all three artefacts; do not move or rename the original
    /home/user/source_logs directory itself.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth files already present before the agent starts
    -->
    <existing_files>
        <file path="/home/user/source_logs/app_server.log">
            2023-03-01 12:00:00 INFO Server started
            2023-03-01 12:05:00 ERROR Failed to connect to DB
            2023-03-01 12:10:00 INFO Retrying...
            2023-03-01 12:10:05 ERROR Retry failed
            2023-03-01 12:10:10 INFO Giving up
            2023-03-01 13:00:00 INFO Daily job executed
            2023-03-01 13:15:00 ERROR Daily job failed
            2023-03-01 13:16:00 INFO Notifying admin
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/source_logs/app_worker.log">
            2023-03-02 08:00:00 INFO Worker started
            2023-03-02 08:01:00 INFO Processing message 1
            2023-03-02 08:01:10 ERROR Message 1 failed
            2023-03-02 08:01:20 INFO Message 1 requeued
            2023-03-02 08:10:00 INFO Worker idle
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/source_logs/app_2022_old.log">
            2022-12-31 23:55:00 INFO Shutdown initiated
            2022-12-31 23:55:05 INFO Waiting for tasks
            2022-12-31 23:55:10 INFO Tasks complete
            2022-12-31 23:55:15 INFO Shutdown complete
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/source_logs/system.log">
            2023-03-03 09:00:00 INFO System check start
            2023-03-03 09:00:10 WARN CPU usage high
            2023-03-03 09:00:20 ERROR Fan failure
            2023-03-03 09:00:30 INFO Fan restart attempt
            2023-03-03 09:00:40 ERROR Fan restart failed
            2023-03-03 09:00:50 INFO Alarm triggered
            2023-03-03 09:01:00 INFO Cooling system engaged
            2023-03-03 09:01:10 INFO Logging event
            2023-03-03 09:01:20 INFO Event logged
            2023-03-03 09:01:30 INFO System check end
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/source_logs/readme.txt">
            These are demonstration logs used by the training task.
        </file>
    </existing_files>

    <!--
        Correct artefacts after agent finishes
    -->
    <expected_outputs>
        <dir path="/home/user/backup_2023" />
        <file path="/home/user/backup_2023/filtered_errors.txt">
            2023-03-01 12:05:00 ERROR Failed to connect to DB
            2023-03-01 12:10:05 ERROR Retry failed
            2023-03-01 13:15:00 ERROR Daily job failed
            2023-03-02 08:01:10 ERROR Message 1 failed
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/backup_2023/backup_report.log">
            app_2022_old.log|4|0|included
            app_server.log|8|3|included
            app_worker.log|5|1|included
            system.log|10|2|skipped
            TOTAL|4|3|4|<ISO_TIMESTAMP>
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/backup_2023.tar.gz">
            # tar archive whose top-level entries are:
            #   source_logs/...
            #     (all five original files exactly as given above)
            #   backup_2023/filtered_errors.txt
            #   backup_2023/backup_report.log
        </file>
    </expected_outputs>

    <!--
        Additional numeric data used by automated tests
    -->
    <counts>
        <total_log_files>4</total_log_files>
        <total_included>3</total_included>
        <total_error_lines>4</total_error_lines>
        <included_files>
            <file name="app_2022_old.log" lines="4" errors="0" />
            <file name="app_server.log"    lines="8" errors="3" />
            <file name="app_worker.log"    lines="5" errors="1" />
        </included_files>
        <skipped_files>
            <file name="system.log" lines="10" errors="2" />
        </skipped_files>
    </counts>
